name: r64_overnight
description: Focused rank-64 overnight sweep. All experiments use rank 64 as base — confirmed best from tier1_thorough early results. 8000 steps to reach convergence (none converged at 4000).

experiments.description: Rank 64 baseline — clean reference at 8000 steps.
Rank 128 — 102GB VRAM makes this free. Does doubling rank from 64 help further?
Rank 64 alpha=32 (scale=0.5). Reduces intruder singular dimensions (arXiv:2410.21228).
Rank 64 alpha=16 (scale=0.25). More aggressive scale reduction — may over-constrain.
LoRA+ ratio=16. lr_B = 16 × lr_A. Faster convergence at constant step budget.
Dropout=0.05. Light sparsity regularisation on LoRA path.
Dropout=0.1. Stronger regularisation — tests if 49 clips needs heavier constraint.
Curriculum sampling: logit_normal for steps 1-4800, then uniform (arXiv:2603.12517).
LR=3e-5. 3× lower — checks if 1e-4 is overshooting at rank 64.
LR=3e-4. 3× higher — may converge faster but risk instability.
Rank 64 targeting attn.qkv + linear1 (FFN projections). Doubles LoRA coverage.
Rank 128 + full target. Maximum possible coverage with available VRAM.
All Tier 1 at rank 64: LoRA+ 16 + dropout 0.05 + curriculum. Full stack at 8000 steps.
Rank 64 alpha=32 + all Tier 1. Best alpha scaling + best regularisation stack.
Rank 128 + all Tier 1. Tests if more capacity + regularisation beats rank 64 full.
